questions.question: Define the probability mass function (PMF) and the probability density function (PDF) and provide an example of each. Explain the difference between them.
Given a discrete random variable X with a probability mass function f(x) = 2x for x = 0, 1, 2, 3, find the probability P(X = 2).
questions.hint: Use the given probability mass function to calculate the required probability

questions.question: A random variable Y has a probability density function f(y) = 1/4y^2 for y > 0. Find the probability P(0 < Y < 1).
questions.hint: Use the given probability density function and the definition of probability density function to calculate the required probability
